news.title: Lucknow: 549 plots allotted under land pooling policy in LDA’s IT City scheme
news.para: The Lucknow Development Authority (LDA) held a lottery to allot residential plots to farmers and landowners who contributed land for the IT City scheme.  The draw, streamed live, allocated 549 plots of various sizes in sectors 15, 16, 17, 18, and 30.  Around 350 landowners pooled nearly 550 acres for the project.

news.title: CIDCO plans 120-acre commercial hub in Navi Mumbai, floats tender for 20-year development
news.para: CIDCO has invited bids to develop a 120-acre International Corporate Park in Navi Mumbai's Kharghar, aiming to create a new business district akin to BKC. This initiative, under the state's Theme-Based policy, seeks a private developer via competitive bidding to foster a world-class corporate ecosystem and boost Navi Mumbai's economic growth.

news.title: Truhome Finance files ₹3,000-crore IPO papers
news.para: Truhome Finance, backed by Warburg Pincus, is launching an IPO to raise Rs 3,000 crore. The housing finance company plans to use the funds to strengthen its capital base for future lending and meet regulatory requirements. This move signals significant growth plans for the affordable housing sector. The IPO includes a fresh issue and an offer for sale.

news.title: Pirojsha Adi Godrej buys nearly 1.8% stake in Arvind SmartSpaces for ₹40.7 crore
news.para: Pirojsha Adi Godrej has acquired a significant stake in Arvind SmartSpaces. He purchased nearly 1.8 percent of the real estate developer's shares. The transaction was valued at Rs 40.7 crore. This deal took place through an open market transaction. An affiliate of HDFC Capital sold the shares.

news.title: Calcutta HC cancels builder's bail, holds liberty of accused cannot be protected by order 'born in sin'
news.para: The Calcutta High Court has cancelled bail granted to a builder in a cheating case. The court found the bail order to be procedurally flawed and lacking in reasoning. Justice Uday Kumar stated that liberty cannot be protected by an order that is 'born in sin'.

news.title: Jewar airport to be inaugurated soon: Civil aviation minister
news.para: Jewar airport in Uttar Pradesh has received its aerodrome licence and will begin operations shortly. The Civil Aviation Minister announced this development in the Rajya Sabha. The government is committed to expanding India's airport network, with plans for new airports and upgrades across the country. This initiative aims to boost regional connectivity and accommodate growing air travel demand.

news.title: Jaipur: About 2,000 Sanganer residents march to protect homes
news.para: Nearly 2,000 residents from 87 Sanganer colonies protested a housing board land acquisition, fearing for their homes. Families have lived in these colonies for years, built houses with bank loans, and developed civic facilities. Residents issued a 15-day ultimatum to the state government for a resolution, threatening intensified agitation if their concerns are not addressed.

news.title: Raymond Realty to jointly develop Mumbai project; County Group unveils ₹1,200 crore project in Noida
news.para: Raymond Realty is developing a new residential project in Kandivali, Mumbai. This project has a gross development value of ₹3,000 crore. Meanwhile, County Group has launched Clove County, a luxury residential project in Noida. This project features 226 apartments with prices ranging from ₹10 crore to ₹18 crore.

news.title: UP-RERA imposes penalty of over ₹2.43 crore on two Lucknow-based builders for failure to upload QPRs
news.para: Uttar Pradesh RERA has fined two Lucknow developers a total of ₹2.43 crore. The penalties stem from repeated failures to upload mandatory quarterly progress reports for their registered projects. This action underscores the authority's commitment to transparency in real estate development. Promoters are urged to comply with RERA regulations to avoid strict regulatory measures and financial penalties.

news.title: Maharashtra government committed to completing Patra Chawl project, resolving residents' issues: Minister
news.para: Maharashtra Minister Shambhuraj Desai assured the state assembly that the government is committed to completing the long-delayed Patra Chawl redevelopment project. Despite concerns over 18 years of delays, incomplete work, and resident protests, Desai stated that all documents are processed and officials are ready to resolve pending issues. A probe into space and leakage complaints will be conducted.

news.title: Lucky numbers and collusion: how an Indian cement cartel came unstuck
news.para: India's largest oil explorer, ONGC, uncovered a decade-long cement price-fixing cartel involving Dalmia Bharat, India Cements, and Shree Digvijay. The companies allegedly colluded on bids, even citing numerology for identical pricing, and attempted to oust foreign competitors. A five-year probe by the CCI found substantial evidence of these anti-competitive practices.

news.title: Goa: Six months jail, ₹10,000 fine if owner fails to keep tenant records
news.para: Goa has implemented the Goa (Verification of Tenants) Rules, 2026, mandating landlords to verify tenant identities and maintain records. Failure to comply can lead to imprisonment up to six months and a fine of Rs 10,000. Police will also maintain inspection records, with provisions for reporting and action against non-compliance or criminal involvement.

news.title: Ranchi civic body cracks down on unlicensed hostels, lodges
news.para: Ranchi Municipal Corporation is cracking down on unlicensed hostels, lodges, and banquet halls, demanding mandatory licenses by March 15. Operators must comply with safety norms, building approvals, and civic regulations or face fines, sealing, and legal action. The RMC emphasizes essential safety features like fire equipment and designated parking for public safety.

news.title: Kolkata civic body seals 15 shops at shopping arcade over property tax dues
news.para: Kolkata Municipal Corporation officials raided Shreeram Arcade, sealing 15 shops for unpaid property taxes. Traders who settled dues reopened their businesses. The civic body recovered Rs 20 lakh and warned that shops not clearing dues by March 31 face auction. Similar drives targeted other areas, highlighting a growing trend of tax evasion by traders in central Kolkata.

news.title: Arnya Real Estate Fund & Supreme Universal secure commitments of ₹1,030 crore
news.para: Arnya Real Estates Fund Advisors and Supreme Universal have secured ₹1,030 crore from investors. This capital is for the Arnya Real Estate Fund-Equity. The fund targets residential redevelopment projects in Mumbai. The launch was in November 2025. The total target corpus is ₹1,250 crore. This partnership brings discipline and transparency to redevelopment.

news.title: Puravankara to jointly develop four acre land in Bengaluru
news.para: Puravankara Group has entered a joint development for a four-acre land parcel on Hennur Road in Bengaluru, with an estimated gross development value exceeding ₹1,300 crore. This move aligns with their strategy of asset-light partnerships in high-potential micro-markets. The project offers 0.84 million sq ft of saleable area and is expected to launch within 6-12 months.

news.title: Haryana backs uniform nomenclature of lands in revenue records
news.para: Haryana is enhancing transparency in land administration by standardizing government land ownership entries in revenue records. Financial Commissioner Dr. Sumita Misra directed officials to uniformly record state government land as 'Haryana government' to eliminate ambiguity and improve governance. This reform ensures legal clarity and accountability in public land management.

news.title: Paperless land surveys by end of year as services go online in Karnataka
news.para: The Survey, Settlement and Land Records wing is set to go fully digital by 2026. Citizens will soon apply for land surveys and get property documents online. This move aims to boost transparency and efficiency. Old records are being digitized. A unique land parcel identification number will be created for each property.

news.title: Land levelled, trees felled, road to new buildings laid at Damdama basin in Aravalis
news.para: Construction and a new road are altering the Damdama Lake catchment, a protected Aravali area. Land has been levelled and fenced, with electricity extended to new structures. This activity threatens Gurgaon's groundwater recharge system. Forest officials will inspect the site for violations. Environmental groups are calling for joint inspections and removal of unauthorised constructions.

news.title: BMC approves empanelment of geotechnical experts to scrutinise foundations of high-rise buildings in Mumbai
news.para: Mumbai's municipal body has mandated independent geotechnical expert scrutiny for high-rise building foundations, a first in Asia. This new norm requires mandatory clearance from the High-Rise Committee for buildings over 180m, ensuring stringent safety standards for deep excavations and complex foundation systems. The move aims to mitigate risks associated with dense urban construction and prevent structural failures.
